lines.en_lit: killing it with an object that is thrown

was taught in accordance with Shmuel: When one kills he should kill it only with a thrown object. one who is rubbed by a mad dog will become dangerously one bitten will die. What is the remedy one who is rubbed by mad dog becomes dangerously ill? Let him take off his clothing and run. Rav Huna, son of Rav Yehoshua, was rubbed by one of these in the market, he took off his clothing and ran. He said: I have fulfilled “Wisdom preserves the lives of those who have it”

One bitten will die. What is the remedy? Abaye said: Let him bring the skin of a male hyena and write on it: I, so-and-so, son of so-and-so, am writing about you upon the skin of a male hyena: Kanti kanti kelirus. And some say Kandi kandi keloros. Yah, Yah, Lord of Hosts, amen amen Selah. And let him take off his clothes and bury them in a cemetery for twelve months the year, he should take them out, and burn them in an oven, and scatter the ashes at a crossroads. And those twelve months the year, when he drinks water, let him drink only from a copper tube lest he see the demon in the water and be endangered, like Abba bar Marta, who is Abba bar Manyumi, whose mother made him a gold tube

And furthermore, Rabbi Matya said: Rabbi Yoḥanan suffered tzefidna, He went to a certain matron [matronita] She prepared a medicine for him on Thursday and Friday. He said to her: What on Shabbat, She said to him: You will not need If I do need what She said to him: Swear to me that you will not reveal He swore: To the God of the Jews, I will not reveal it. went out and taught it publicly,
But he swore to her I will not reveal it to the God of the Jews. I will reveal it to His people, the Jews. Still, there is a desecration of God’s name, He revealed to her at the outset.

What she prepared for him? Rav Aḥa, son of Rav Ami, said: water leaven olive oil, and salt. Rav Yeimar said: leaven itself, olive oil, and salt. Rav Ashi said: fat a goose’s wing. Abaye said: I made all of these and was not cured until a certain Arab told me Take olive seeds that are less than one-third and burn them in a fire on top of a new hoe, and stick them along the row of gums. I did this and was cured.

From where this wheat bread that is too hot and fish remains fried in oil. What is the sign of When one puts something between his teeth, blood comes out from his gums. When Rabbi Yoḥanan suffered from tzefidna, he prepared this on Shabbat and was cured. And how did Rabbi Yoḥanan prepare this Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: Tzefidna is different, since it does begin in the mouth but it ends up in the intestines

Rav Ḥiyya bar Abba said to Rabbi Yoḥanan: In accordance with whose Rabbi Matya ben Ḥarash, who said: one who suffers pain in his mouth, one puts medicine in his on Shabbat, said to him: but I say in this but no other.

Let us say that supports him: one who is seized with yerakon, one feeds him donkey meat one whom a mad dog bit, one feeds him the lobe of its liver; one who has pain in his mouth, one puts medicine in his on Shabbat; this is the statement of Rabbi Matya ben Ḥarash. And the Rabbis say: These have no as a remedy. these, to exclude what? What, is it not to exclude medicine

No, it excludes Bloodletting serunkhi So too, this is reasonable as it was taught Rabbi Yishmael, son of Rabbi Yosei, said three things that he heard in the name of Rabbi Matya ben Ḥarash: One let blood for serunkhi on Shabbat; and one whom a mad dog bit, one feeds him the lobe of its liver; and one who has pain in his mouth, one puts medicine in his on Shabbat.
And the Rabbis say: These have no as a remedy. these to exclude what? What, is it not the latter two to exclude the first No, to the first two first and excludes the latter clause
